signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical De-escalation + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.133 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite US-Iran MOU headlines driving a broader risk-on environment. Price is holding above the 160.00 level, which previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk is acute on a sustained break higher. JPY COT LF net short -115,036 (long% 29.2%) remains crowded, while 25d RR at -1.10 confirms the options market is paying for downside protection against BoJ action. Carry dominates the flow until VIX > 25, but geopolitical de-escalation reduces safe-haven JPY demand.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3970 — retail sentiment is 91% short, diverging sharply from the COT LF net short of -60,671 (long% 24.7%). This institutional vs retail alignment on the short side suggests a crowded trade, yet the pair remains supported by a +150bp carry advantage. USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has broken to -0.228 (z=-1.61σ), indicating the pair is no longer responding to the 5.63% drop in WTI. Retail capitulation risk is elevated if price extends toward 1.4000.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Carry Neutrality
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3424 — IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 6.0% generates a +5.0pt VRP, the highest in the G10 space. Options markets are pricing significant protection ahead of the June 18 BoE meeting despite a neutral 0bp carry spread (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%). COT LF net long +21,961 (61.7%) shows specs are positioned for GBP strength, but the 25d RR at -0.71 indicates a growing put skew. Elevated vol premium suggests the market is hedging for a hawkish disappointment or USD rebound.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning Stalling + Vol Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1617 — COT LF net short -49,290 (long% 37.4%) reflects a bearish institutional bias, yet the pair is up 0.38% intraday on US-Iran MOU headlines. IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.4% (+2.7pt VRP) indicates the options market is paying a premium for protection over realized moves. While the +150bp USD carry advantage structurally limits upside, the positive CESI for USD (+24) vs EUR (+2) suggests data outperformance may eventually reassert USD dominance. Watch for a break of 1.1650 to invalidate the bearish positioning read.
